titleStmt.title: SII 25.234: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 234. (A.R. No. 234 of 1908.) BHAIRAMGARH, BASTAR STATE, CENTRAL PROVINCES.

summary.p: Slab setup near the police station, being brought from Jaṅgla-Pōtēnār.
This epigraph furnishes the details of date viz., Śaka 982, Sārvari, Kārttika śu 5, Monday corresponding to 1060 A.D., October 2.

It states that the king was camping at Bārasūru. A feudatory of his was the mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Chaṁdrādityarāju, who is stated to have belonged to the Solar race (dinakara-kula), to the Kāśyapa-gōtra and to the Karikāla family. It also mentions that this chief was the lord of Kāvvāri (i.e., Kāvēri). that his crest was a lion, that he was the lord of Oṟayūra (Uṟaiyūr), that he was born of the Chōḷa-vaṁśa and that he was the chief of the village Ammagāma. The portion mentioning the titles, etc and the grant details are the same as the ones found in another record of the same king. (No. 231).

The introductory portion referring to the Chōḍa chief Chandrāditya is identical with that of the Telugu-Chōḍa chiefs of the Cuddapah region. Both appear to have had their origin from a common stock and thus claims to have belonged to the family of Karikāla (Karikāl-ānvaya).

It states that the chief Chandrāditya had built a tank called Chandrāditya-samudra at his capital Bārasūra (Barsūr) and on its bank constructed a temple to Śiva whom he had set up there in the name of Chandrādityēśvara. He is stated to have granted lands in Vaḍḍa-nāṇḍu as dēvabhōga to this temple with the permission of Dhārāvarusa (i.e., Dhārāvarsha).
This Dhārāvarusa is identical with the Sinda king Jagadēkabhūshaṇamahārāja Dhārāvarsha.
The sthānapati of this temple Dharmarāśi is stated to have established a flower-garden, named after the chief Chandrāditya.
The witnesses to the endowment are Budda-nāyakuṇḍu, Suṁkaya-Dēma-nāyakuṇḍu, the rājaguru Rudrapaṇḍita, Komāra Nārāyaṇaśeṭṭi, Aniyaṁka Mallaśeṭṭi, and Aṁgaḍi Duggiśeṭṭi.
Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. X, No. 645.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti sahasra phaṇā
maṇi kiraṇanikarāpabhāsura
nāgavaṁsōdbhava bhōgāvatīpuraparēśvara
viśadajaya paṭupaṭa gāṁbhīryya dhvānālaṁkā
rita daśadiśāṁtarāladhēnu vyāghralāṁchana
mahēśvaracaraṇa ka
ritabhramarāya mā
jagadēkabhūṣaṇa mahārā
vṛddhi pravarddhamāna mācaṁdrārkka
nāṇḍubārasūri nelavīṭa sukha
saṁkathā vinōdamulanḍugā śakanripakālā
tīta saṁvatsaraṁbulu 982 nēṇṭiśārvvari
saṁvatsaraṁbuna kārttika māsaṁbuna śuddha paṁ
cami sōmavāramu nāṇḍu svasti Ari
durddharavarabhujāsibhāsura pracaṇḍa
pradyōtkara dinakarakulanandana kāsya
pagōtra karikālanvayakāvyārinātaka
ṁbalapaṟeghōṣaṇa raktadhvaja siṁhalāṁcha
na Oṟayūra puravarēśvaracōḍavaṁ
sōdbhavaṁ AmmagāmaWritten below the line.puravarēśvarulaina
śrīmanmahāWritten below the line.maṇḍalēśvaraWritten below the line.caṁdrādityarājulu

rājadhāni
bārasūra
damacēyiṁ
cina caṁdrādi
tya samudra
nāmāṁkita
taṭākaṁbuna
śētānana da
ma pratiṣṭa sē
sina caṁdrādi
tyēśvaraṁbaina I
śvarālayaṁbu
naku śrīmaddhārā
varusarājula
cēta nēnusē
viṁccinilcikonna
di vaḍḍa naṇḍunu
nūru bhōgā

bhyaṁtara siddhāyā
caṁdrārkkamu dēva bhōgaṁ
bugānicci nilpina śi
lāsthaṁbhasāsanamu Ivvidha
ṁbuna niddēvālayaṁbunaku
sthānapati dharmmarāsi dattaṭākasi
vālaya samīpaṁbuna nijasthāpi
taṁbaina caṁdrādityu nandanavasaṁbu
nu dāniki Iccinadi Illugā dīnikiṁga
mmalanāṇḍu buddanāyakuṇḍunu karaṇa
mu mī nāyakuṇḍu suṁkayadēma nā
yakuṇḍu srīkaraṇamu mināyakuṇḍu rāja
gurulu rudrapaṇḍitulu komāra nārāyana
seṭṭiyu naniyaṁka mallaseṭṭiyu Aṁggaḍi cuggiseṭṭiyu
vīru sākṣi puruṣulugā nīsvarayavrālu dīni
ṁgāla kālāṁtaramuṁ jananīkanatmaṁgādani srijiyi
ṁcina bāhattariniyōgamula Aṁdevvaṇḍēni
kalgenēni naddi jalpiviI kaṁbhasthiti seṟpi
na rāju galgenēni vāraṇāsi vēya gavilala
vēvuru brāhmala jaṁpina netturu drāginavāru
kāṁpulukoṁdaṟina bekkeṇḍuna Ariga
ccina ma nu daṇḍa dēsamulē

svadattaṁ paradattaṁ
vāyōharēti
vasundhara ṣaṣṭi
ṁ varuṣa saha
srāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ
jāyatē krimi
khaṇḍariṁcina vā
ṇḍu bhīmācari
I kaṁbhasthitineṟpi
ṁcinavāru vāraṇā
sini Adharmmuṇḍe
ṁci Ivariyu
gavilā boḍci ne
tturu dāginavāru
Digital edition of SII 25.234 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
